creator: ashish@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to t-matubara from comment #0)
> editors/emacs23 EXPIRATION_DATE to 2014-11-19
> Please cancel.
> 
> It is because emacs23 is used now.
> 
> While using editors/emacs(24.4), the following cannot use it by a compile
> error.
> 
> editors/apel
> 
> editors/flim      (For editors/apel compile error)
> editors/semi      (For editors/apel compile error)
> japanese/navi2ch  (For editors/apel compile error)
> 
> <Environment>
> [uname -a]
> FreeBSD tamago-2.tamago.local 10.1-PRERELEASE FreeBSD 10.1-PRERELEASE #0
> r274176: Fri Nov  7 01:59:14 JST 2014    
> matubara@tamago-2.tamago.local:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/TAMAGO-2  amd64
> 
> [ports]
> Revision 372304
> 
> 
> Best regards

Hi,

Emacs 23 is not supported upstream, and gets texinfo errors during building, so the best course of action, I think is to remove it from ports.

I'm wondering if you could test out apel[1] (from Wanderlust repository), and see if it works for you, then maybe we can ask MANTANI-san (maintainer of editors/apel, added to this bug thread) to switch to that instead.

[1] https://github.com/wanderlust/apel/

Thanks,
Ashish
